nodes.label: AI as skill expander and learning tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as task automator that does work for me
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want AI to just 'do it for me' I need to know the how

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for content creation
nodes.label_negative: AI as content generator lacking nuance
nodes.grounding_span: I ask the AI to help me with the outline and proofing, but the words are mine

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability and need for verification
nodes.grounding_span: I don't quite trust it, I have to always ask for sources

nodes.label: Human-crafted value vs. machine-produced output
nodes.label_negative: Machine-produced output as cheap and lacking value
nodes.grounding_span: we still pay more for handcrafted items, they are still viewed with more value than a machine made item

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner vs delegator
nodes.label_negative: AI as independent delegate for complete tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I collaborate - my tasks are intensive, I couldn't delegate

nodes.label: Organizational productivity vs. job replacement risk
nodes.label_negative: Job replacement risk from AI-driven productivity
nodes.grounding_span: they don't know that they could be replacing staff with this progress

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal integrity
nodes.grounding_span: the words are mine, they carry intention

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I have to always ask for sources, and open another chat to make the same request for data to check its answers

nodes.label: Authentic skill and craftsmanship
nodes.grounding_span: we still pay more for handcrafted items

nodes.label: Security and organizational integrity
nodes.grounding_span: they could be replacing staff with this progress

nodes.label: Positive stance toward AI as research tool
Negative stance toward AI-generated writing
nodes.grounding_span: Reading AI content lacks the nuance of human written words

nodes.label: Valuing human skills over AI output
nodes.grounding_span: I will always value human skills more

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing jobs
nodes.grounding_span: it scares me a bit, I feel that they don't know that they could be replacing staff

nodes.label: Iterative collaborative problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: I collaborate - my tasks are intensive, I couldn't delegate, its more of process to work through

nodes.label: Verification-oriented approach
nodes.grounding_span: I have to always ask for sources, and open another chat to make the same request for data to check its answers
